author.headline: Chief Executive Officer @ Founders Verbinden | Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning

posted_at.relative: 1 week ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

text:
Just published something I've been thinking about for a while. 🚀
While going through my Software Engineering coursework — SDLC, Requirement Engineering, Agile, the V-Model, DevOps — I kept noticing the same pattern.
Everyone talks about using AI to write code.
Nobody talks about using it to engineer systems.
And there's a real difference.
A vague prompt like "build a food delivery app" gives you random code that works until it doesn't.
But what if you approached it the way a senior architect would?
"Act as a senior software architect. Build a scalable food delivery platform with JWT authentication, role-based access, modular backend, CI/CD, Docker support, a testing strategy, database schema, API docs, and a full deployment workflow. Generate: SRS → System Design → Folder Structure → REST APIs → Testing Plan → Deployment Architecture."
That single shift changes AI from an autocomplete tool into an actual engineering assistant.
I explored this in my latest article — diving into why Software Engineering fundamentals matter more in the AI era, not less. Topics include Multi-Agent AI systems, AI-Augmented SDLC, AI Technical Debt, DevOps automation, and where things are heading with Large Action Models.
The core argument: when code generation becomes cheap, engineering judgment becomes expensive.
The engineers who will thrive aren't the ones generating the most code — they're the ones who understand architecture, requirements, and system design well enough to direct AI with precision.
I'd genuinely love to hear from people actually in the field:
Has AI improved your engineering workflow — or has it mostly just accelerated output without improving the underlying architecture quality?
Drop your honest take in the comments. 👇
#SoftwareEngineering #AI #SystemDesign #DevOps #SoftwareArchitecture

author.headline: Chief Executive Officer @ Founders Verbinden | Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning

article.title: AI Is Rewriting Software Engineering — But Most Developers Still Think Like It's 2015
article.subtitle: Anant Kumar

posted_at.relative: 3 weeks ago • Edited • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

text:
Building in silence. Launching with clarity.
Over the past few weeks, I’ve been working on something that started as a simple idea:
👉 “Why is gifting still confusing, slow, and generic?”
That question turned into a system.
Not just a website — but a decision-driven custom gifting platform designed to remove friction from:
• Choosing a gift
• Personalizing it
• Delivering it on time
We broke it down to fundamentals:
Occasion → Recipient → Product → Customize → Deliver
No noise. No guesswork. Just execution.
What I learned during this process:
Most platforms focus on design, not decisions
Personalization without real preview breaks trust
Speed (same-day / fast delivery) is not optional anymore
Simplicity converts more than features
We’re building with a clear principle:
👉 If it doesn’t help the user decide faster, it doesn’t belong.
This is just the beginning.
The goal is to evolve this into a full emotional commerce system, not just another gifting site.
Currently working on:
• Customization engine (live preview)
• Smart recommendation system
• Seamless checkout + delivery flow
If you’re interested in:
E-commerce, UI/UX systems, or building real products — let’s connect.
More updates soon.
#startup #buildinginpublic #ecommerce #uxdesign #productdevelopment #entrepreneurship

text:
Most people don’t lack discipline.
They lack alignment.
As an author, I didn’t want to write another “feel-good” book.
I wanted to build something that actually changes how you function — biologically, mentally, and practically.
Because the real problem is this:
We are trying to live high-performance lives… on disrupted internal systems.
Late nights.
Irregular sleep.
Constant stimulation.
And then we blame ourselves for low energy.
So I went deeper.
I studied circadian biology, neuroscience, and ancient systems like Ayurveda — not to collect knowledge, but to extract a working model.
That model became:
📘 The Rhythm Code
This book is built like a system, not a story.
Inside, you’ll find:
• How your circadian rhythm dictates energy, focus, and recovery
• Why consistency beats intensity (biologically)
• Practical protocols — morning, evening, breathing resets
• A 7-day reset to regain control
• A 30-day structure to rebuild long-term energy
This is not about doing more.
It’s about doing in sync.
If you feel like your effort ≠ output,
this is the layer you’re missing.
🔗 Read here: https://amzn.in/d/03DEKr5u
If you do read it, approach it like a system —
test, observe, adapt.
That’s where transformation actually happens.

author.headline: Chief Executive Officer @ Founders Verbinden | Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning

posted_at.relative: 3 months ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

text:
🌿 Introducing EcoVulnAI — AI-Assisted Ecological Vulnerability Assessment Platform
I’m excited to share my college Environmental Studies (CA Project) — EcoVulnAI, a web-based ecological vulnerability screening system designed to support early-stage environmental risk assessment.
🔗 Live Project:
https://lnkd.in/grpqMZ7P
🌍 What is EcoVulnAI?
EcoVulnAI calculates an Ecological Vulnerability Index (EVI) using a structured multi-criteria model inspired by the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP).
It evaluates projects based on:
• Project Impact
• Species Threat Level
• Habitat Fragmentation
• Ecosystem Sensitivity
The system then classifies the project into:
🟢 Low Risk
🟡 Moderate Risk
🟠 High Risk
🔴 Critical Risk
and generates a policy recommendation accordingly.
🚀 Key Features
✔ Interactive Ecological Assessment Dashboard
✔ Radar Chart Risk Visualization
✔ Compare Two Projects Mode
✔ Scenario Simulation (Future Risk Projection)
✔ Satellite-Based Vegetation Analysis (NDVI Concept)
✔ Interactive Risk Map
✔ Downloadable PDF Assessment Report
✔ Dark Mode Interface
Built using:
• HTML, CSS, Vanilla JavaScript
• Chart.js for data visualization
• Netlify Serverless Functions (Node.js backend)
🎯 Why This Project?
Environmental Impact Assessments are often conducted late in planning cycles. EcoVulnAI explores how AI-assisted screening tools can support early-stage ecological decision-making while remaining transparent and interpretable.
The goal is not to replace formal EIA processes, but to provide a structured decision-support framework aligned with sustainable development principles.
👨‍💻 Developed by: Anant Kumar
Team Members: Kunal Singh, Siddhivinayak Mishra
As part of my College CA Project in Environmental Studies.
I would truly appreciate feedback from professionals working in sustainability, biodiversity conservation, environmental governance, or AI for climate solutions.
#EnvironmentalStudies #Sustainability #ArtificialIntelligence #Biodiversity #ClimateAction #SDG15 #StudentProject #EcoVulnAI

text:
🎉 Excited to Introduce ShareMaison! 🎉

As a B.Tech AI/ML student at Lovely Professional University, I'm thrilled to launch ShareMaison – an innovative all-in-one rental platform designed specifically for students. This community makes borrowing, lending, and sharing resources like gadgets, books, and gear easier, more efficient, and enjoyable, helping you navigate college life with greater convenience.

Whether you're looking to share your items, find the perfect gear for your next project or event, or contribute ideas to enhance the platform, your participation is key.

Take just 2 minutes to complete this quick survey and help shape ShareMaison into the ultimate student-friendly rental solution: https://lnkd.in/ejQg2DXi

Your insights will drive smarter, simpler, and more social renting experiences. Thank you for joining the ShareMaison community – let's build something great together! 🙌

#StudentLife #Entrepreneurship #RentalPlatform #CollegeInnovation #ShareMaison #LPU
